items.title: Testing a pure chunker
items.scenario: `chunk_text(text, max_tokens)` splits a string into chunks no larger than `max_tokens`.
items.explanation: Test empty input, single-token input, exact-boundary input, and an oversized input that must split into N chunks. All pure logic, all milliseconds — perfect unit-test territory.

items.title: Testing retry/backoff without sleeping
items.scenario: Your `call_with_retry` retries on 429 with exponential backoff up to 60 seconds.
items.explanation: Inject a fake clock (or use `freezegun`). Mock the API to return 429 twice then 200. Assert the retry sequence and the cumulative backoff — without waiting 60 real seconds.

items.title: Parametrized boundary tests
items.scenario: Tokenizer should handle 0, 1, max, max+1 tokens.
items.explanation: `@pytest.mark.parametrize('n', [0, 1, 4095, 4096, 4097])` runs the same test logic with each input — 5 tests for ~10 lines of code.

items.title: Regression test for a fixed bug
items.scenario: Bug: `dedup_chunks` lost the last chunk when the input ended exactly on a boundary.
items.explanation: Add a test reproducing the exact input that triggered the bug. The test fails *first*, then you fix the bug. Now the test prevents the bug from coming back.
